# Civilizational composite diagnostic (issue #4 Path A)

Composite period: **1994-12-31 → 2024-10-31** (359 monthly observations).
Construction: equal-weight mean of rolling 120m z-scores of
GINIALLRF (annual, shifted +9 months for publication lag),
USEPUINDXM (monthly), and −UMCSENT (monthly, inverted).

## Lead-lag correlation profile (vs S&P 500 monthly returns)

| Lag k (months) | Interpretation | corr(composite_t, return_{t-k}) |
|---|---|---|
| -24 | composite leads by 24m (returns realised after t) | +0.069 |
| -12 | composite leads by 12m (returns realised after t) | +0.023 |
| -6 | composite leads by 6m (returns realised after t) | -0.014 |
| -3 | composite leads by 3m (returns realised after t) | -0.020 |
| +0 | coincident | -0.097 |
| +3 | composite lags by 3m (returns realised before t) | -0.192 |
| +6 | composite lags by 6m (returns realised before t) | -0.102 |
| +12 | composite lags by 12m (returns realised before t) | -0.083 |
| +24 | composite lags by 24m (returns realised before t) | -0.053 |

S&P 500 drawdown threshold for episodes: **-15%**.

## Drawdown episodes (>15% S&P 500 declines)

| Start | Trough | Recovery | Trough DD | Composite −12mo | Composite −6mo | Composite at trough | Composite +12mo |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1998-07-31 | 1998-08-31 | 1998-11-30 | -15.6% | -0.69 | -0.26 | +0.01 | -0.44 |
| 2000-09-30 | 2002-09-30 | 2007-05-31 | -46.3% | -0.51 | -0.50 | +1.25 | +0.97 |
| 2007-11-30 | 2009-02-28 | 2013-03-31 | -52.6% | +0.53 | +0.63 | +2.08 | +1.17 |
| 2020-01-31 | 2020-03-31 | 2020-07-31 | -20.0% | +0.61 | -0.07 | +1.50 | +0.55 |
| 2022-01-31 | 2022-09-30 | 2023-12-31 | -24.8% | +1.17 | +0.80 | +1.70 | +0.49 |

## Interpretation

By peak absolute correlation, the composite at month t is most strongly related to equity returns realised 3 months **before** t — i.e., the composite is **lagging** past returns (peak at lag k=+3, corr=-0.192). Coincident corr is -0.097; corr at k=−12 (composite leading future 12m returns) is +0.023; corr at k=+12 (composite lagging past 12m returns) is -0.083.

Composite latest reading: **+0.12** (2024-10). Historical high: **+2.36** (2008-10).

**Caveat (sample-scope memory):** This characterisation rests on a single sample — US equities, 1975-present. The composite has lived through ~50 years and a handful of large drawdowns; that's a small number of independent episodes for any signal claim. The dynamic could differ in other countries, other asset classes, or under structurally different macro regimes. Treat this as a hypothesis for further stress-testing, not a proven leading indicator.

## Method

- **Composite**: `src.indicators.internal_order_stress_index` with
  `publication_lag_months=9`, `zscore_window=120` (10y monthly).
- **Inputs**: GINIALLRF (annual, shifted forward 9 months for typical
  Census/World Bank release lag), USEPUINDXM (Baker-Bloom-Davis EPU,
  monthly), UMCSENT (UMich consumer sentiment, monthly, inverted so
  higher = more stress).
- **Drawdown**: `price / running_peak − 1` on month-end S&P 500.
- **Episodes**: contiguous below-peak runs whose minimum DD ≤ -15%.
- **Lead-lag correlation**: `corr(composite_t, return_{t-k})`.
  Negative k means the composite leads (correlates with future returns,
  realised after t); positive k means it lags (correlates with past
  returns, realised before t).
